opp.name: BarbizonLighting_Opp_S2C_April'26
opp.next_step: JRA 5/20: Email to Chris to get demo video feedback.
opp.ais_why:
• On the April 14 intro Chris Clark engaged warmly with the Merlin Risk Radar pitch, asking about AI-driven monitoring of supplier financial health and geopolitical risks.
• Discussion centered on supplier risk and CLM use cases, not on autonomous negotiation or intake, so AI interest is real but narrow.
• Next step is passive - Justin sending Showpad demos; no commitment to a deeper agentic conversation yet.

items.quote: No Economic Buyer, CFO, procurement director, or C-level sponsor named or introduced on the 14 April discovery call; Christopher Clark (Regional Manager) is an Evaluator, not a budget owner.

items.quote: Single-stakeholder discovery call with no process map; Christopher committed to review videos and respond but did not describe next steps, approvals, or who else needs to be involved.

items.quote: Christopher described manual data entry errors, duplicates, and contract red-lining pain but did not quantify cost, cycle time, or savings target.

sources: Avoma intro call 14 April 2026, Christopher Clark statement on manual data entry errors and duplicates
Avoma notes: 'Reduction of manual data entry errors and duplicate records to improve procurement process efficiency and data quality' (implied requirement)
metrics.narrative: Christopher Clark articulated operational pain around manual data entry errors and duplicate records in their Sage ERP procurement process (source: Avoma 14 April, Chris: 'Of course, errors and duplicates, you know, manual data entry'). However, the buyer did NOT quantify the impact in financial terms (no cost savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, or error-rate baseline stated). The pain is qualitative and confirmed but not yet tied to a business case with ROI metrics. Making the business case quantifiable is a critical next step to move this deal forward and secure Economic Buyer sponsorship.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Christopher Clark (Regional Manager, Purchasing & Warehousing)
Avoma 14 April: Christopher engaged cooperatively in discovery but did not advocate or introduce other stakeholders
SF NextStep 20 May: rep sent demo videos; buyer committed to review and respond but has not done so (37 days silent)
champion.narrative: No champion secured. Christopher Clark (Southeast Regional Purchasing and Operations Manager) is the single contact and was engaged and responsive on the 14 April discovery call, but he has gone silent since the 20 May demo video handoff (37 days no reply). He has not introduced Zycus to his director (who owns contract pain), to the Economic Buyer, or to IT (Vincent Syrek, Director of IT, is on the account but not confirmed involved in this opp). Christopher agreed to review the demo videos and provide feedback on whether to explore further, but that commitment has not been fulfilled. His engagement was cooperative but transactional (he answered discovery questions but did not advocate, share internal docs, or drive the agenda forward). Without a response to the demo videos and with no multi-threading to other stakeholders, Christopher is currently a WEAK, at-risk contact, not a champion. Re-engaging him with a value-led follow-up and securing an introduction to his director or the budget owner are the immediate priorities.

sources: Avoma 14 April, Christopher Clark: 'We do. Yes. Spend analysis, invoicing, procurement, reporting.' (confirming Sage ERP covers most needs)
Avoma 14 April: Christopher identified supplier risk management and contract management as gaps, not a wholesale Sage replacement
SF Competitors field: unknown (no external vendor competition recorded)
No RFP, shortlist, or vendor comparison mentioned on the 14 April call
competition.narrative: Barbizon is NOT running a competitive bake-off at this stage; the deal is NOT benchmarking Zycus against Coupa, SAP Ariba, or Jaggaer. The ONLY 'competitor' is DO-NOTHING: Barbizon already uses Sage ERP 2025 for procurement and Salesforce for supplier information management, and Christopher Clark confirmed on the 14 April call that they have MOST Source-to-Pay functionality covered in Sage ('Do you have most of these functionalities covered in Sage at this point?' / Chris: 'We do. Yes. Spend analysis, invoicing, procurement, reporting'). The buyer sees gaps in supplier risk management and contract lifecycle management, which is why they are exploring Zycus, but they have not stated any urgency to replace Sage or named a compelling event driving a purchase decision. The real competitive threat is INERTIA: Barbizon continues using Sage ERP with workarounds for risk and contract pain, and Zycus fails to build a quantified business case that justifies displacing the status quo. Winning this deal requires converting the operational pain (errors, duplicates, contract red-lining, risk blindness) into an executive-sponsored, ROI-backed initiative with a named Economic Buyer and a mutual close plan.

sources: Avoma 14 April, Christopher Clark: 'Of course, errors and duplicates, you know, manual data entry'
Avoma 14 April, Christopher Clark on contract red-lining: 'Not me specifically, but my director. She definitely does.'
Avoma 14 April, Christopher Clark: 'Supplier risk... that's just the kind of the global landscape that we're in right now... these past 4 or 5 years for sure.'
identify_pain.narrative: Christopher Clark, Southeast Regional Purchasing and Operations Manager, clearly articulated two operational pain points on the 14 April discovery call. First, manual data entry errors and duplicate records in their Sage ERP 2025 procurement process are causing inefficiency and data quality issues (Chris: 'Of course, errors and duplicates, you know, manual data entry'). Second, his director is stuck in a back-and-forth contract red-lining cycle with legal ('my director... definitely does' deal with '8 iterations of red lines'), slowing contract negotiation. Third, Barbizon sees a gap in proactive supplier risk management given the global risk landscape over the past 4-5 years (Chris: 'Supplier risk... that's just the kind of the global landscape that we're in right now'). The pain is CONFIRMED and articulated by the operational owner (Christopher Clark), but it has not yet been escalated to or quantified by an Economic Buyer or C-level sponsor, so the business case and urgency remain unvalidated at the executive level.

sources: Avoma 14 April: no paper process or contracting mechanics discussed
Christopher Clark mentioned Barbizon uses Sage ERP 2025 and Salesforce, implying integration touchpoints, but no formal scoping or security review initiated
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, or InfoSec process discussed. The 14 April call was scoping-focused (pain discovery and demo video handoff) with no conversation about MSA, SOW, redlining, security review, or procurement approval mechanics. Christopher Clark mentioned Barbizon uses Sage ERP and Salesforce with some integration between them, implying a tech stack integration requirement, but no formal scoping or InfoSec review timeline was discussed. Paper process visibility will only emerge once the deal advances beyond discovery and a commercial conversation begins with the Economic Buyer and legal/procurement stakeholders.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Christopher Clark (Regional Manager, Purchasing & Warehousing)
Avoma 14 April: Christopher Clark described his role and team structure but did not name or introduce a budget owner or executive sponsor
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer or budget authority identified. Christopher Clark is the Southeast Regional Purchasing and Operations Manager (Evaluator role per SF contact role) and has a team under him, but he has not mentioned or introduced a C-level sponsor, CFO, or procurement director who controls the commercial decision for a $120k Source-to-Pay platform purchase. The 14 April call was entirely operational (pain discovery and demo scoping) with no discussion of budget, approval process, or executive sponsorship. Barbizon is a multi-region operation (Atlanta, Orlando, Virginia, Charlotte mentioned); a deal of this size likely requires director or VP-level sign-off, but that stakeholder has not surfaced. Mapping the Economic Buyer is the single highest-priority move to advance this deal from Qualified to a credible forecast.

sources: Avoma 14 April: single-stakeholder call, no process discussion
SF OpportunityContactRole: only 1 buyer contact (Christopher Clark) mapped, below multi-thread bar
decision_process.narrative: No decision process mapped. The 14 April call was a solo discovery conversation with Christopher Clark (Evaluator); no other Barbizon stakeholders joined, and no approval chain, timeline, or evaluation stages were discussed. Christopher mentioned his director handles contracts, suggesting a multi-level decision structure, but he did not describe the steps Barbizon will take to evaluate Zycus (demo, POC, vendor comparison, legal review, executive sign-off). The buyer committed to review demo videos and 'get back to Justin if it's worth exploring further', but there is no mutual close plan, no named next milestone, and no visibility into who else needs to be involved or when a decision will be made. The process is entirely unknown beyond the initial discovery phase.

sources: Avoma 14 April, Christopher Clark: 'Supplier risk, you know, that's just the kind of the global landscape that we're in right now... these past 4 or 5 years for sure. So, yeah, that would be a huge help.'
Avoma 14 April: Chris confirmed interest in contract management to help his director handle the 'back and forth email game with legal and 8 iterations of red lines'
Avoma notes: 'Desire for an intuitive, user-friendly eProcurement experience embedded in communication tools like Microsoft Teams or Slack to increase adoption'
decision_criteria.narrative: Christopher Clark explicitly prioritized two evaluation criteria on the 14 April call: supplier risk management (proactive flagging of financial, geopolitical, operational risks) and contract lifecycle management (AI-driven clause analysis to reduce negotiation cycles). He confirmed Barbizon already has most Source-to-Pay functionality covered in Sage ERP 2025 but sees gaps in risk monitoring and contract red-lining. He also expressed interest in an intuitive eProcurement experience embedded in Teams or Slack to increase adoption. However, no formal RFP, shortlist, or weighted scorecard has been shared, and the buyer has not stated whether Zycus is being compared against other vendors or is sole-sourced. The criteria are IMPLIED from the discovery conversation, not yet formalized or confirmed in a written business requirements document.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Christopher Clark expressed genuine interest in AI-driven supplier risk management on the 14 April call, describing it as 'a huge help' given the global risk landscape of the past 4-5 years. He also acknowledged the value of AI contract clause analysis to reduce his director's red-lining burden. The buyer is AI CURIOUS (sees practical value in AI for operational pain) but has not articulated a strategic AI transformation agenda or C-level AI mandate. The AIS fields are null in Salesforce, so there is no formal AI excitement score recorded, but the Avoma call signals a pragmatic, use-case-driven AI appetite (risk and CLM) rather than AI resistance or hype-driven enthusiasm.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 15 April 2026 (1 day after the intro call) and has been in Qualified stage for 72 days. Single discovery call 14 April (~10 weeks ago) with Christopher Clark; Zycus committed to send demo videos, rep followed up 20 May per NextStep, but buyer has not responded (37 days silent). LastActivityDate 15 June (11 days ago) shows rep is maintaining the record but no buyer-facing activity since 20 May. No follow-up call scheduled, no additional stakeholders engaged, no commercial or EB conversation initiated. Deal is stalled waiting on buyer feedback.

items.name: Christopher Clark
items.risk: At risk—single contact, no multi-threading, and buyer has not fulfilled commitment to review videos and provide feedback. If Christopher does not re-engage, the deal stalls indefinitely.
items.title: Regional Manager, Purchasing & Warehousing (Southeast region: Atlanta, Orlando, Virginia, Charlotte)
items.sentiment: Initially engaged and cooperative on the 14 April discovery call (articulated pain, confirmed interest in risk and CLM, agreed to review demo videos), but has gone silent for 37 days since the 20 May video handoff with no response or follow-up scheduled.

items.detail: No Economic Buyer identified; Christopher Clark is an Evaluator (Regional Manager), not a budget owner or C-level sponsor. A $120k Source-to-Pay purchase requires director or VP-level sign-off, but that stakeholder has not surfaced. Without executive sponsorship and a quantified ROI case, this deal will not advance beyond Qualified.

Single-threaded to Christopher Clark with no multi-threading to his director (contract pain owner), IT (Sage/Salesforce integration), or procurement leadership. If Christopher disengages or deprioritizes Zycus, the deal has no alternate advocate or path forward.

Buyer has not responded to demo videos sent 20 May (37 days overdue). Christopher committed to review and provide feedback on next steps but has gone silent. Without re-engagement, the deal is stalled indefinitely and the 2027-03-18 close date (265 days out) becomes a placeholder, not a plan.

No budget discussion, no ROI case, no quantified savings target. Christopher articulated operational pain (manual data entry errors, contract red-lining, supplier risk gaps) but did not quantify the financial impact or confirm budget availability for a $120k purchase. Without a business case, the deal cannot secure Economic Buyer sponsorship.

Barbizon uses Sage ERP 2025 for procurement and Salesforce for supplier information ('they kind of talk to each other, essentially'). Zycus will need to integrate with both systems to maintain supplier master records and procurement data consistency. IT (Vincent Syrek, Director of IT) has not been engaged to scope integration complexity, timeline, or InfoSec requirements.

Incumbent inertia: Barbizon already has MOST Source-to-Pay functionality in Sage ERP 2025 (spend analysis, invoicing, eProcurement, reporting per Christopher 14 April). Zycus is positioned as a GAP-FILLER for risk and CLM, not a full Sage replacement. The competitive threat is DO-NOTHING: Barbizon continues with Sage workarounds and deprioritizes Zycus if the pain is not quantified and escalated to an Economic Buyer.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator), Avoma intro call 14 April 2026, SF NextStep 20 May (no buyer response for 37 days)
champion_strength.summary: No champion. Christopher Clark (Southeast Regional Purchasing Manager, single contact) was engaged and cooperative on the 14 April discovery call but has gone silent for 37 days since the 20 May demo video handoff. He has not advocated for Zycus, introduced other stakeholders (his director, IT, or Economic Buyer), or driven the agenda forward. His engagement was transactional (answered discovery questions, agreed to review videos) but not champion-level. Without a response to the videos and with no multi-threading, Christopher is a WEAK, at-risk contact, not a champion.

items.action: Re-engage Christopher Clark with a geo-specific risk event story to secure his director introduction and restart momentum.
items.trigger: Buyer silent for 37 days since 20 May demo video handoff; committed to review and respond but has not done so.
items.expected_effect: Re-ignites buyer interest by tying Zycus Merlin risk radar to a tangible, recent supply-chain disruption in the Southeast US lighting/entertainment sector that Barbizon's Sage ERP would not have flagged proactively. Positions the follow-up as value-led (not a passive check-in) and gives Christopher a business reason to re-engage and introduce his director (the contract pain owner). If Christopher replies, secure a call with him and his director within 10 days to validate CLM and risk fit and map the Economic Buyer.

items.action: Secure an introduction to Christopher Clark's director to validate contract lifecycle management pain and map the Economic Buyer.
items.trigger: Christopher mentioned his director handles contracts and 'definitely does' deal with the back-and-forth red-lining pain, making her a key CLM stakeholder and likely gatekeeper to the Economic Buyer. She has not been introduced or engaged.
items.expected_effect: Converts a single-contact, stalled discovery into a multi-stakeholder evaluation. The director is the pain owner for CLM (the #2 use case Christopher prioritized) and likely reports to or has access to the procurement/finance leader (Economic Buyer) who controls the $120k budget. Engaging her validates the CLM business case, de-risks single-contact dependency, and opens the path to Economic Buyer identification and a quantified ROI discussion.

items.action: Co-author a quantified ROI case with Christopher and his director tying Zycus to cost savings from error reduction and contract cycle-time compression.
items.trigger: Christopher articulated operational pain (manual data entry errors, duplicates, contract red-lining) but did not quantify financial impact or savings target. No business case, no Economic Buyer sponsorship, no path to close.
items.expected_effect: Converts qualitative pain into a quantified, executive-grade business case that justifies the $120k investment and secures Economic Buyer sponsorship. The ROI case should quantify: (1) cost of manual data entry errors and duplicates in Sage ERP (labor hours, rework, order delays), (2) contract cycle-time savings from AI-driven clause analysis (reduce director's red-lining burden by 50%+, accelerate time-to-signature), and (3) risk mitigation value (proactive supply-chain risk flagging prevents disruptions). This case becomes the artifact that moves the deal from Qualified to Shortlisted and arms the Economic Buyer to secure internal approval.

items.action: Multi-thread to IT to scope Sage ERP and Salesforce integration requirements and timeline.
items.trigger: Barbizon uses Sage ERP 2025 and Salesforce with some integration between them. Zycus will need to integrate with both to maintain supplier master records and procurement data consistency. IT (Vincent Syrek, Director of IT on the account) has not been engaged.
items.expected_effect: De-risks technical feasibility and surfaces integration complexity, InfoSec requirements, and timeline early (before contracting). Engaging IT (Vincent Syrek or equivalent) ensures Zycus integration with Sage and Salesforce is scoped and approved by the technical gatekeeper, preventing a late-stage integration blocker. IT engagement also multi-threads the deal beyond Christopher and his director, raising deal resilience.

items.action: If no reply from Christopher within 10 business days of the rank-1 outreach, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive connect.
items.trigger: Buyer silent for 37 days since 20 May; rank-1 re-engagement attempt by deal owner. If Christopher does not reply within 10 business days, the deal is at risk of going fully dark and needs executive intervention.
items.expected_effect: Escalates the relationship to peer-level (manager-to-director or manager-to-VP) to re-open the conversation at a higher altitude. The executive connect should position the outreach as a strategic check-in on Barbizon's supplier risk and contract management priorities (not a 'why haven't you replied' message) and offer a brief executive briefing on how Zycus is helping similar lighting/entertainment enterprises proactively manage supply-chain disruptions. This gives the deal a second chance to re-engage before it goes fully dark.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is COOLING after a single discovery call 10 weeks ago and 37 days of buyer silence since the 20 May demo video handoff. Christopher Clark was engaged and interested on the 14 April call (articulated pain, confirmed interest in risk and CLM, agreed to review videos), but he has not responded or scheduled a follow-up. No multi-threading, no Economic Buyer mapped, no commercial conversation initiated. The deal is stalled waiting on buyer feedback, and without re-engagement it will slip into dark territory.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 15 April 2026 (72 days in Qualified stage); close date 2027-03-18 is 265 days out. Time-in-stage is appropriate for early Qualified (discovery phase), and the close date is not aggressive. Last verified activity 15 June (11 days ago) suggests rep is keeping the record warm, but the last BUYER engagement was the 14 April intro call (~10 weeks ago). Rep reached out 20 May (per NextStep) with demo videos; awaiting buyer reply for 37 days. The buyer committed to review videos and respond with interest level but has not done so. One genuine stalled action (waiting on buyer feedback to advance) puts this deal in Slowing, not On Track.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early Qualified deal (9 months out) has stalled 37 days awaiting buyer feedback on demo videos sent 20 May. Single-threaded with Christopher Clark who went silent after 14 April discovery call; no follow-up meeting scheduled, momentum lost.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal (Qualified, 72 days in stage) with strong product fit on risk and CLM but stalled after a single discovery call and 37 days of buyer silence. Three best practices to raise win probability: (1) re-engage Christopher Clark with a value-led follow-up tying Zycus risk radar to a recent supply-chain disruption story (geo-specific to Southeast operations) to re-ignite interest and secure his director intro; (2) map the Economic Buyer immediately (a $120k Source-to-Pay purchase requires director or VP-level budget authority, and Christopher is an Evaluator, not a decision-maker); (3) multi-thread beyond Christopher to his director (contract pain owner), IT (Sage/Salesforce integration stakeholder), and the procurement leadership to de-risk single-contact dependency and build a coalition.

competitive_position.summary: No external vendor competition. The only 'competitor' is DO-NOTHING / status quo: Barbizon uses Sage ERP 2025 for procurement (covers spend analysis, invoicing, eProcurement, reporting per Christopher Clark 14 April) and Salesforce for supplier information. Buyer sees gaps in supplier risk management and contract lifecycle management but has not stated urgency to replace Sage or named a compelling event. The competitive threat is INERTIA: Barbizon continues with Sage ERP workarounds and Zycus fails to build a quantified, executive-sponsored business case. Winning requires converting operational pain into a strategic initiative with an Economic Buyer and ROI justification.

competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of manual data entry errors and duplicate records in Sage; position Zycus Merlin risk radar as a proactive supply-chain safeguard Sage cannot deliver; demonstrate AI-driven contract clause analysis to cut the director's red-lining cycle time by 50%+; secure Economic Buyer sponsorship with a documented ROI case (savings from error reduction + risk mitigation + contract cycle-time compression) that justifies displacing the Sage status quo.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Strong initial positioning, not yet converted to executive sponsorship or action
ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning was STRONG on the 14 April call and resonated with Christopher Clark's pain points. Justin Ajmo led with Zycus's 8-year AI model development, highlighted Merlin risk radar (proactive supply-chain risk flagging) and AI-driven contract clause analysis (reducing red-lining cycles), and Christopher explicitly confirmed interest in supplier risk management given the global risk landscape ('Supplier risk... that's just the kind of the global landscape that we're in right now... these past 4 or 5 years for sure. So, yeah, that would be a huge help.'). The AI narrative was the PRIMARY differentiator vs Sage ERP (which covers transactional procurement but lacks proactive risk intelligence and contract AI). However, the positioning has not yet been VALIDATED by the Economic Buyer or converted into a quantified ROI case, and the buyer has not re-engaged since the video handoff, so the AI story has not yet driven forward motion beyond initial interest.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong initial fit on the two pain points Christopher Clark prioritized: supplier risk management (Zycus Merlin risk radar proactively flags financial, geopolitical, operational risks, which resonated with Chris given the global risk landscape) and contract lifecycle management (AI-driven clause analysis to reduce the director's red-lining burden). Barbizon already covers most Source-to-Pay needs in Sage ERP, so Zycus is positioned as a GAP-FILLER for risk and CLM, not a full ERP replacement. Integration with Sage ERP and Salesforce is an implicit requirement. Buyer expressed interest in an intuitive eProcurement experience embedded in Teams/Slack to drive adoption, which aligns with Zycus guided buying functionality. No formal BRD, RFP, or weighted scorecard shared, so fit is inferred from the discovery conversation, not validated against a written requirements document.

hard.ais_why:
• On the April 14 intro Chris Clark engaged warmly with the Merlin Risk Radar pitch, asking about AI-driven monitoring of supplier financial health and geopolitical risks.
• Discussion centered on supplier risk and CLM use cases, not on autonomous negotiation or intake, so AI interest is real but narrow.
• Next step is passive - Justin sending Showpad demos; no commitment to a deeper agentic conversation yet.
hard.next_step: JRA 5/20: Email to Chris to get demo video feedback.

deltas.subject: Re-engage the stalled buyer contact with a geo-relevant, value-led outreach. Christopher Clark has been silent for 37 days since the 20 May demo video handoff. The highest-leverage move is a targeted follow-up tying Zycus Merlin risk radar to a recent, REAL supply-chain disruption in the Southeast US lighting/entertainment supply chain (e.g. a supplier financial distress, a geopolitical tariff impact, a logistics delay) that Barbizon's current Sage ERP would NOT have flagged proactively. Position the follow-up as 'I saw this risk event affecting Southeast operations and thought of our 14 April conversation—here's how Merlin would have alerted you 30 days ahead.' This re-anchors the conversation to tangible business value, not a passive 'just checking in', and gives Christopher a reason to re-engage and introduce his director.
